tools.name: lookup_mac_serial
tools.description: Check a Mac serial number: whether a verified Macfax report exists for it, plus the model and year decoded from Apple's data for pre-2021 serials. 2021+ Macs have 10-character randomized serials that encode nothing, so for those the honest answer is the format check and the report cross-reference. L

tools.name: get_mac_price_stats
tools.description: What a used Mac configuration actually sells for AND what sellers are asking right now. The sold slice is the headline: verified-sale percentiles when the config has enough recent sales (sold.basis 'direct'; n and window ride along), else the asking band scaled by a measured clearance ratio (sold.ba

tools.name: search_mac_listings
tools.description: Live used-Mac listings aggregated across eBay, Craigslist, OfferUp, Swappa, Facebook and Reddit, with scam clusters, junk titles, classified-ad bait, auctions and stale/sold rows already filtered out. Every result deep-links to the source listing where the purchase happens. Filter by configuration,

tools.name: check_mac_listing
tools.description: The trust picture for one specific listing, by URL (eBay/Craigslist/OfferUp/Swappa/Facebook/Reddit) or Macfax listing id: whether Macfax knows it, whether it still passes every quality gate, scam/junk/classified/auction flags, when a scan last verified it live, its ask against the configuration's ty

tools.name: get_mac_report
tools.description: A verified Macfax condition report as structured data: hardware-verified identity, Activation Lock / MDM / serial-match checks, coverage status, and the signing chain. Use when a listing or seller shares a macfax.com/r/ link and the buyer wants the facts behind it. status "superseded" means the same

tools.name: create_mac_alert
tools.description: Watch the market for a configuration: Macfax emails the given address when new matching listings appear. CONSENT CONTRACT: create an alert only for a user who explicitly asked for this alert on this email address. The first alert for a new email stays inactive until the email's owner clicks the conf

findings.title: Toxic flow: untrusted input can reach an external sink
findings.detail: Server "mcp" exposes tools that together enable an exfiltration flow. Untrusted content: check_mac_listing · Public sink: create_mac_alert · Destructive: lookup_mac_serial.

evidence.snippet: Untrusted content: check_mac_listing · Public sink: create_mac_alert · Destructive: lookup_mac_serial

findings.remediation: Do not co-install these capabilities under one trust boundary; an injected comment/email can steer the model to exfiltrate via the sink tool.

findings.title: Tool description changed since last scan
findings.detail: Tool "get_mac_price_stats" changed its description with no version change. Silent description changes are how a trusted server turns malicious after review.

evidence.snippet: before: "What a used Mac configuration is listed for right now AND what it actually sells for. Returns the asking band (median/p25/p75 with sample size), per-channel medians with net-to-seller after fees, launch MSRP retention, Apple trade-in floor, and a sold slice: verified-sale percentiles when the config" → after: "What a used Mac configuration actually sells for AND what sellers are asking right now. The sold slice is the headline: verified-sale percentiles when the config has enough recent sales (sold.basis 'direct'; n and window ride along), else the asking band scaled by a measured clearance ratio (sold.ba"

findings.remediation: Re-review the tool before continuing to trust it.
